title: Pre-Populate Form Fields based on from the URL it was opened

posts.author: brandonf (Brandon Ferrar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello!

It would be very help to auto-populate fields within a form that is opened from a specific URL.

For example we are implementing a form within Salesforce that allows users to submit a ticket in Monday. Within the form they need to specify the tech stack they are having the issue in since we have several different ones (Salesforce, Hubspot, etc.).

It would be great for Salesforce or Hubspot to pre-populate in the form if the link was opened from those sites without having users to fill in that info.

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @brandonf

We just officially launched URL Parameters in our monday form builder Easyform, check out our community post here.

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Brandon! Shannon here with the monday team. Thank you for submitting this feature request. I’m happy to share that within the question settings for WorkForms, users can now set pre-fill values! Please note that the “URL parameter” pre-fill option is only available to users on the Pro and Enterprise Plan tiers at this time.

Check out this article for more details about the feature: WorkForms question types > Default question settings.
